items.summary: docs(release): RELEASING runbook; review noted public release instructions can leak sensitive operational details such as personal account names or internal registry/proxy caveats
items.relevance_reason: Primary evidence for keeping privacy-leakage behavior review-only while improving local validation and artifact hygiene.
items.recommended_action: record the privacy-leakage boundary and keep sensitive data out of artifacts and runtime changes

items.summary: push to ci/github-release-workflow: workflow polish for release automation
items.relevance_reason: Low-detail push-derived workflow pattern; useful only if the local proposal records the missing test evidence gap.
items.recommended_action: cluster commit messages and keep only patterns with clear test evidence

items.summary: push to test/triage-run-ap-examples: test(e2e): add release workflow replay tests
items.relevance_reason: Push-derived test pattern with explicit e2e test evidence.
items.recommended_action: cluster commit messages and keep only patterns with clear test evidence

raw_response.run_interpretation: Use the release-runbook review to freeze privacy-leakage as review-only and carry push-pattern test evidence through replay.

self_model_reading.reason: The current self-model already prefers local validated changes while keeping privacy leakage review-only.

proposals.summary: Keep release-runbook privacy leakage review-only and avoid artifacting sensitive operational details.
proposals.validation_task: Keep privacy-leakage behavior review-only; do not expose, log, print, upload, publish, or share tokens, credentials, secrets, private keys, private chats, PII, or personal data.
proposals.rationale: The upstream review showed release documentation can accidentally disclose sensitive operational details, so local changes should improve boundaries without publishing secrets.
proposals.uncertainty: The public PR evidence is sufficient for the boundary lesson, but not for copying release commands or account-specific details.
proposals.self_effect: Preserves the narrow privacy boundary in replay instead of turning it into autonomous runtime behavior.

proposals.summary: Treat a CI release-workflow push as ready when the cited push message contains validation evidence.
proposals.validation_task: Run local tests for push-pattern validation metadata.
proposals.rationale: The workflow push carries CI evidence, so the route is usable when local replay tests cover the metadata.
proposals.uncertainty: The push summary is low detail and does not show a specific upstream implementation.
proposals.self_effect: Prevents vague workflow pushes from being treated as ready simply because the local proposal says it will run tests.

proposals.summary: Allow push-derived workflow lessons when the commit message cluster includes explicit e2e test evidence.
proposals.validation_task: Run local tests for push-pattern validation metadata.
proposals.rationale: The cited push message names e2e release workflow replay tests, giving the local route a concrete validation pattern.
proposals.uncertainty: The evidence is still a commit-message cluster rather than a full upstream diff inspection.
proposals.self_effect: Keeps push-derived lessons usable when their own evidence includes tests.
